title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

description: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a beloved classic in Chinese bakeries, renowned for its soft, fluffy bread and distinctive crispy, sweet topping. Despite its name, it contains no pineapple; the topping simply resembles the fruit's skin texture. This delightful pastry offers a perfect balance of textures and flavors, making it an irresistible treat for breakfast or a snack.

ingredients.notes: for bun dough, plus 1 for egg wash

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 250g all-purpose flour, 30g granulated sugar, 3g instant dry yeast, and 2g salt. Mix well. Add 1 beaten egg and 100ml warm milk. Knead until a rough dough forms. Add 30g softened unsalted butter and continue kneading for 10-15 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size. This step is crucial for soft buns.

instructions.title: Prepare the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream together 60g softened unsalted butter and 60g powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon evaporated milk until fully combined. Gradually add 120g all-purpose flour and mix until a soft, uniform dough forms. Cover and refrigerate.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the bun dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.

instructions.title: Apply the Topping
instructions.text: Take the topping dough from the refrigerator. Divide it into 8 equal portions. Flatten each portion into a thin disc, about 4-5 inches in diameter. Gently place each topping disc over a bun dough ball, molding it slightly to cover the top. Score a crisscross pattern on the topping with a knife.

instructions.title: Second Proofing and Bake
instructions.text: Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise again for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F). Brush the exposed bun dough edges and the topping with an egg wash (1 egg beaten with a splash of milk). Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the Pineapple Buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm for the best taste and texture. Enjoy your homemade Bo Lo Bao!

tips: For an extra fluffy bun, ensure your milk for the dough is warm, not hot, to activate the yeast properly. Around 40-45°C (105-115°F) is ideal.
If you dont have evaporated milk for the topping, regular whole milk can be used as a substitute, though evaporated milk provides a slightly richer flavor.
Leftover Pineapple Buns can be stored at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days. Reheat briefly in a toaster oven for a crispy topping.
article:
Unlock the secrets to a beloved Chinese bakery classic and bring the comforting aroma of freshly baked goods into your home. We're talking about the iconic Pineapple Bun, known in Cantonese as Bo Lo Bao (菠蘿包). This isn't just any bun; it's a culinary masterpiece that perfectly balances a soft, fluffy interior with a sweet, crumbly, and slightly crispy topping. Despite its name, there's no actual pineapple in this delightful treat; the name comes from the distinctive crisscross pattern on its golden-brown crust, which resembles the skin of a pineapple.

Originating from Hong Kong, the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao has captivated taste buds worldwide, becoming a staple in dim sum restaurants and bakeries. Its unique texture and comforting sweetness make it an ideal snack, breakfast item, or accompaniment to a cup of milk tea. While it might seem intimidating to make at home, with the right guidance, you can recreate this authentic taste in your own kitchen. Get ready to embark on a delicious baking adventure as we guide you through everything you need to know to master the art of making the perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

The Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a quintessential item in Chinese baking, especially beloved in Hong Kong-style bakeries. Its name, "Bo Lo Bao," directly translates to "Pineapple Bun" in Cantonese. This nomenclature stems purely from its appearance – the cracked, golden-brown topping, once baked, forms a mosaic pattern reminiscent of a pineapple's rough skin. Crucially, the bun contains no pineapple fruit whatsoever, a common misconception for those unfamiliar with this treat.

At its core, the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao consists of two distinct components that create its signature appeal. First, there's the bun itself: a rich, tender, and slightly sweet yeast dough, often enriched with milk and eggs, giving it a wonderfully soft and airy texture similar to a brioche or milk bread. Second, and perhaps the most defining feature, is the "pineapple" topping. This is a sugary, buttery, cookie-like crust that’s spread thinly over the dough before baking. As it bakes, this topping expands and cracks, creating the characteristic pattern and providing a delightful contrast of textures – the crisp, crumbly exterior against the cloud-like interior. The magic lies in this textural interplay and the harmonious blend of sweet, buttery flavors.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao requires a few key ingredients, divided between the dough and the iconic topping. Quality ingredients will make a noticeable difference in the final product.

For the Bun Dough:
*   3 cups (360g) Bread Flour (high-protein flour is best for chewiness)
*   1/4 cup (50g) Granulated Sugar
*   1 teaspoon Salt
*   2 1/4 teaspoons (7g) Instant Dry Yeast
*   1 large Egg, at room temperature
*   3/4 cup (180ml) Warm Milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C)
*   1/4 cup (56g) Unsalted Butter, softened

For the Pineapple Topping:
*   1/2 cup (113g) Unsalted Butter, softened
*   1/2 cup (100g) Granulated Sugar (or powdered sugar for a finer crumb)
*   1 large Egg Yolk (or a whole egg for a slightly softer topping)
*   1 1/2 cups (180g) All-Purpose Flour
*   1 tablespoon Milk Powder (optional, for richness)
*   1 teaspoon Custard Powder (optional, for color and classic flavor)
*   1/2 teaspoon Baking Powder

For the Egg Wash:
*   1 large Egg, beaten with 1 tablespoon water

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao from scratch is a rewarding process. Follow these steps carefully for the best results.

I. Prepare the Bun Dough:
1.  In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread flour, sugar, salt, and instant dry yeast. Whisk well to ensure even distribution.
2.  In a separate bowl, whisk together the warm milk and egg.
3.  Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
4.  Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 5-7 minutes until it starts to come together.
5.  Add the softened unsalted butter and continue kneading for another 8-10 minutes. The dough should become smooth, elastic, and pass the windowpane test (stretch a small piece until it's thin enough to see light through without tearing).
6.  Form the dough into a ball, place it in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

II. Make the Pineapple Topping:
1.  While the dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream together the softened unsalted butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2.  Beat in the egg yolk until well combined.
3.  In a separate bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, milk powder (if using), custard powder (if using), and baking powder.
4.  Gradually add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture, mixing until just combined and a soft, pliable dough forms. Be careful not to overmix.
5.  Form the topping dough into a log, wrap it in plastic wrap, and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to firm up. This makes it easier to handle.

III. Shape the Buns:
1.  Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air.
2.  Divide the dough into 10-12 equal pieces (about 60-70g each) and roll each piece into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each.
3.  Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of pieces as your buns.
4.  On a lightly floured surface or between two pieces of parchment paper, roll out each piece of topping dough into a thin circle, large enough to cover the top of a bun.
5.  Carefully place a rolled topping circle over each bun, gently pressing it down to adhere. Use a small knife to lightly score a crosshatch pattern on the surface of the topping, resembling a pineapple.

IV. Second Proofing and Baking:
1.  Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let them proof again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
2.  Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
3.  Just before baking, gently brush the surface of the pineapple topping (avoiding the sides of the bun) with the egg wash for a golden shine.
4.  Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown and the topping is crisp and fragrant.
5.  Transfer the baked Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving bakery-quality Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Don't Rush the Kneading:** Thorough kneading is crucial for developing gluten, which gives the bun its characteristic soft, chewy texture. A stand mixer with a dough hook can make this process easier.
*   **Patience with Proofing:** Allow the dough to proof fully in both stages. A warm, humid environment (like a slightly warmed oven turned off) can help. Under-proofed dough will result in dense buns, while over-proofed dough can collapse.
*   **Chill the Topping:** Chilling the topping dough is essential. It makes it less sticky and easier to roll out thinly without tearing. If it becomes too soft while working, pop it back in the fridge for a few minutes.
*   **Handle Topping Gently:** When placing the topping on the bun, handle it gently. You want it to cover the top without stretching it too much, which could cause it to crack excessively during baking.
*   **Light Scoring:** Score the topping lightly but distinctly. A too-deep score can cause the topping to fall off, while a too-shallow one won't create the "pineapple" pattern effectively.
*   **Even Baking:** Rotate your baking sheet halfway through the baking time to ensure even browning and crispness of the topping.

## Serving Suggestions

The best way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is warm, fresh from the oven, when the contrast between the crispy topping and soft bun is at its peak.

*   **Classic Hong Kong Style:** Slice the bun horizontally and insert a thick slab of cold butter. The butter melts into the warm bun, creating a decadent, savory-sweet experience (known as Bo Lo Yau).
*   **With Tea or Coffee:** Pair it with a classic Hong Kong-style milk tea, strong black coffee, or even just a glass of milk for a comforting breakfast or afternoon snack.
*   **Plain and Simple:** The bun is so delicious on its own that it needs no embellishment. Just tear into it and savor the flavors and textures.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a star in itself, there are several delicious variations and substitutions you can explore:

*   **Filled Pineapple Buns:** For an extra treat, add a filling to your buns before the second proofing. Popular choices include a slice of cold butter (Bo Lo Yau), sweet red bean paste, creamy custard, or even savory BBQ pork (though this makes it a distinct "Cha Siu Bo Lo Bao").
*   **Flavorful Topping:** Experiment with the topping by adding a touch of vanilla extract for aroma, or a hint of lemon zest for a bright, citrusy note. Some recipes also use a small amount of condensed milk in the topping for added richness.
*   **Whole Wheat Flour:** For a slightly healthier twist, substitute a portion of the bread flour with whole wheat flour. This will result in a denser bun, but can add a nice nutty flavor.
*   **Dairy-Free Options:** Use plant-based milk and butter substitutes to make the buns suitable for those with dairy allergies or dietary preferences. Ensure your yeast is also dairy-free.
*   **Smaller Buns:** Divide the dough into smaller portions for mini Pineapple Buns, perfect for parties or as bite-sized treats. Adjust baking time accordingly.

Mastering the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a journey into the heart of Chinese baking. With these detailed instructions and helpful tips, you're well on your way to creating these iconic, irresistible treats right in your own kitchen. Enjoy the process, savor the aroma, and delight in the incredible taste of your homemade Bo Lo Bao!
